accepted.reason: Array-backed tree growth, split indexing, and traversal logic are strong structured-kernel examples.
Point sampling over deterministic and disk distributions gives compact geometric generation kernels.
Nested-sequence checks and allocation/free callback wrappers capture useful low-level utility patterns.
Wall potential force and torque equations provide concise physics-oriented numeric kernels.
Reindex, gather, and scatter routines are broadly reusable index-manipulation optimization targets.
Board-feature flattening and label construction produce practical dense feature-engineering loops.
Bloom-filter sizing math and seed generation offer high-signal probabilistic data-structure kernels.

skipped.reason: Cython lifecycle semantics test coverage, not standalone computational kernel logic.
Argument-passing behavior tests are language conformance checks, not conversion targets.
Primarily model-definition/wrapper plumbing with little independent numeric-kernel content.
